title: stachys recta subsp. hirta
intro: Stachys recta subsp. hirta, also known as Hairy Woundwort, is a plant species within the genus Stachys, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce pain and swelling. The health benefits of Hairy Woundwort include its ability to soothe digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve respiratory function. Hairy Woundwort can be prepared as an herbal tea or infused in oil for topical use, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies for various health concerns.
